acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Canonical store state lives in `StoreRuntime`, not `RepoState`.
- [ ] Git lane metadata lives in `GitLaneState` (renamed file/module).
- [ ] No name collision with `src/repo.rs`.
- [ ] Tests pass.
description:
**Problem**
- `RepoState` owns canonical `StoreState` plus git lane metadata, mixing concerns.
- `StoreRuntime` holds `repo_state`, so store state is indirectly coupled to git operations.
- `RepoState` name conflicts with `src/repo.rs` and is easy to open by mistake.

**Files:**
- src/daemon/repo.rs
- src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
- src/daemon/core.rs
- src/git/sync.rs (only if types are referenced)
design:
- Move `StoreState` ownership into `StoreRuntime` (`state: StoreState`).
- Rename `RepoState` to `GitLaneState` (or `CheckpointLaneState`) and keep only git/checkpoint metadata there.
- Update `Daemon` maps to store `GitLaneState` separately from store runtime.
- Remove unused fields like `wal_sequence` if they are now owned by WAL.
- Update all references/imports.
title: Decouple StoreState from RepoState (rename RepoState)

description:
**Problem**
After phased migration, residual handler files in beads-rs can linger and create dual ownership/scatter.

**Design**
Switch final beads-rs CLI dispatch/entrypoint to beads-cli-owned command tree and delete migrated handler files/modules from beads-rs. Keep binary behavior and output compatibility unchanged.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] beads-rs no longer owns migrated CLI command handler modules
- [ ] Command tree ownership is singular under beads-cli
- [ ] cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration -- cli::critical_path:: passes

**Files:** crates/beads-rs/src/cli/*, crates/beads-cli/src/commands/*, crates/beads-rs/src/bin/main.rs

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: Completed wrapper-finalization pass: removed dual ownership for migrated read-only command handlers by deleting beads-rs wrappers (list/search/ready/blocked/stale/count), routing dispatch directly to beads-cli handlers, and updating render_query to beads-cli render helpers. Kept behavior unchanged (same underlying handlers/arg types). Validation: cargo check -p beads-rs; cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration -- cli::critical_path:: (6/6 pass).

notes.author: darin@darinsmcstudio2.lan
notes.content: Completed wrapper-finalization pass: removed dual ownership for migrated read-only command handlers by deleting beads-rs wrappers (list/search/ready/blocked/stale/count), routing dispatch directly to beads-cli handlers, and updating render_query to beads-cli render helpers. Kept behavior unchanged (same underlying handlers/arg types). Validation: cargo check -p beads-rs; cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration -- cli::critical_path:: (6/6 pass).

title: Finalize CLI crate ownership and delete beads-rs command handlers

closed_reason: Switched tests to thread-local override_data_dir_for_tests; removed lock usage and added allow(dead_code) on lock APIs.
description:
**Problem**
Unit tests that override data dir grab a global lock (paths::lock_data_dir_for_tests) and serialize across the suite. This blocks parallel test execution and shows up as time spent waiting.

**Design**
Use thread-local DataDirOverride (paths::override_data_dir_for_tests) in unit tests instead of global set_data_dir_for_tests + lock. Update affected tests to use RAII guard. Keep lock-based API for cases that truly need cross-thread overrides.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Tests no longer use lock_data_dir_for_tests when not needed
- [ ] Data dir override is scoped and does not leak
- [ ] Tests still pass in parallel

**Files**: src/git/checkpoint/cache.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs, src/daemon/core.rs
title: Remove global test data dir lock by using thread-local overrides

description:
**Problem**
Log level/filter is only controlled by `LOG` env or `-v`, so daemon log verbosity is not persistently configurable.

**Design**
- Add `logging.filter` (string) to config schema for `tracing_subscriber::EnvFilter` directives.
- Add env override `BD_LOG_FILTER` (string). Precedence: LOG env > BD_LOG_FILTER > config.logging.filter > verbosity.
- Update `telemetry::init` to build an EnvFilter that merges the chosen source.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Config accepts `logging.filter = "beads_rs=debug,metrics=info"`.
- [ ] `BD_LOG_FILTER` overrides config, `LOG` overrides both.
- [ ] Tests updated or added for parsing/precedence.

**Files:** src/config/schema.rs, src/config/merge.rs, src/telemetry.rs, src/bin/main.rs
title: Observability: configurable log filter/level

description:
**Problem**
\`Bead\` in beads-rs doesn't carry a namespace field (namespace is on the event); \`DepKey\` in \`crates/beads-core/src/dep.rs\` doesn't either. Cross-namespace dep edges are silently allowed but **graph traversal in one namespace cannot see beads in another**.

This is the PRIMARY blocker for the sessions and extmsg namespaces (docs/go-parity/primitives/namespaces.md Deliverable 1). Session beads in the \`sessions\` namespace need to reference Workflow beads in \`core\`; Binding beads in \`extmsg\` need to reference Session beads in \`sessions\`.

Without this fix, a \`bd dep list <session-id>\` returns an empty list even if a dep exists to a \`core\` bead, because traversal is single-namespace.

Agent-4 research output: docs/go-parity/primitives/namespaces.md §1.

**Design**
Add \`namespace: NamespaceId\` to \`DepKey\` (both \`from\` and \`to\` sides, since either endpoint could be in a different namespace than the \"local\" one).

\`\`\`rust
pub struct DepKey {
    pub from: BeadId,
    pub from_namespace: NamespaceId,
    pub to: BeadId,
    pub to_namespace: NamespaceId,
    pub kind: DepKind,
}
\`\`\`

Propagate through:
- \`CanonicalState\` dep storage (\`namespaced_state.rs\`)
- Dep-add / dep-remove / dep-list paths
- Wire ops in \`wire_bead.rs\`
- Any graph-traversal primitive (e.g., blocked-by computation in \`bd ready\`)

**Migration:** existing DepKey without namespace defaults to the bead's home namespace on deserialization. Fresh writes always carry both namespaces explicitly.

**Test coverage:** add a test that creates two beads in different namespaces, links them via \`bd dep add\`, and verifies \`bd dep list\` traversal returns the cross-ns edge.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] \`DepKey\` struct includes \`from_namespace\` and \`to_namespace\`
- [ ] Dep traversal in \`CanonicalState\` returns edges regardless of target namespace (with appropriate ACL/visibility checks if those land later; for now, no check)
- [ ] Wire serialization preserves both namespaces
- [ ] Migration/backward-compat: existing single-namespace-implicit deps decode to the bead's namespace
- [ ] Unit test: dep from core → sessions namespace is reachable from either side
- [ ] Integration test: \`bd dep list <id-in-sessions> --json\` returns edges to core beads once the sessions namespace ships (hypothetical, since it doesn't yet — test the mechanism with two core sub-namespaces instead)
- [ ] \`cargo xtest\` green, \`just dylint\` green, \`cargo clippy\` green

**Independent of**
Other Floor 1 work. This is a core-crate schema change and wire format touch.

**Unblocks**
Sessions namespace, extmsg namespace (both Floor 3).

**Files likely touched**
- \`crates/beads-core/src/dep.rs\` (DepKey definition)
- \`crates/beads-core/src/namespaced_state.rs\`, \`crates/beads-core/src/state/\`
- \`crates/beads-core/src/wire_bead.rs\`
- \`crates/beads-core/src/apply.rs\` (dep apply ops)
- \`crates/beads-cli/src/commands/dep.rs\`
- Migration tests + new cross-namespace integration test
title: Floor 1: cross-namespace DepKey (add namespace field to dep edges)
